title: Learning Waypoints — Unified Node File Schema
description: Subject-agnostic schema for all standard node map files across Science, Maths, DT, English and future subjects. One file per ACARA/IB/curriculum standard. Replaces earlier subject-specific schemas.

year_level.description: Year level string. e.g. '7', '7-8' for banded curricula.

title.description: Short human-readable title. e.g. 'Classification and Biodiversity'

strand.description: Optional. Curriculum strand label. e.g. 'Understanding', 'Processes and Production', 'Human Endeavour', 'Inquiry Skills', 'Number and Algebra'. Free text — not constrained to a fixed enum to allow cross-subject use.

mode.description: Optional. Planning mode. A = concept-certifying (shared conceptual sequence, assessment independent of task). B = capability-certifying (process/inquiry sequence, thinking only legible through the work). Defaults to A for U-strand standards.

prior_knowledge.description: What students are assumed to know before this unit. Derived from curriculum progression chain.

progression_endpoint.description: Fixed conceptual endpoint for all students — the shared assessment target. Framed as 'Students...'. Replaces y_goal. Must not be exceeded by any node or width tier.

nodes.description: Ordered sequence of conceptual waypoints from assumed prior to progression_endpoint. All nodes are mandatory for all students.

id.description: Sequential node number within this standard. Starts at 1.

label.description: Short display name for the node. e.g. 'Purpose of classification'

progression_checkpoint.description: Conceptual position reached at this node — framed as 'Students can...'. This is where students are at this waypoint on the progression toward the endpoint. Replaces y_description.

minimum_width.description: Minimum construction required before advancing to the next node. Non-negotiable floor — certifies readiness. Set by the command term of the descriptor. Replaces xmin.

typical_width.description: Full descriptor scope. Standard construction for a typical class at this node. Broader integration than minimum_width but same concept. Replaces width_core.

wider.description: Enrichment directions — extended phenomena, contexts or transfer tasks. Each item is a discrete enrichment direction. Must add phenomena or context — not depth-on-fewer-phenomena. Example directions only, not exhaustive. Replaces width_enrich_options.

hinge.description: True if this is a hinge concept — a node where misconceptions cluster, downstream nodes depend on it, and width investment pays off disproportionately.

hinge_reason.description: Required if hinge is true. Explains why this is a hinge — dependency chain, misconception gateway, assessment sensitivity. Null if hinge is false.

success_criteria.description: 2-3 assessable evidence statements anchored to minimum_width threshold. Observable, measurable indicators of what a teacher looks for to confirm the waypoint is met. Width-agnostic — same criteria for all students regardless of width level.

related_standards.description: Optional. Related curriculum codes — inquiry skills, human endeavour, cross-strand links. Used for future lesson prompt enrichment and sharing ecosystem filtering. Replaces inquiry_links and endeavour_links.

notes.description: Optional. Free-text notes for curriculum designers — flags non-standard decisions, known misconceptions, pedagogical caveats.

_naming.progression_endpoint: Replaces y_goal — the fixed ACARA/curriculum endpoint for the unit
_naming.progression_checkpoint: Replaces y_description — the node-level conceptual position
_naming.minimum_width: Replaces xmin — the non-negotiable floor before advancing
_naming.typical_width: Replaces width_core — standard construction for typical class
_naming.wider: Replaces width_enrich_options — enrichment directions, not harder versions of typical
_naming.related_standards: Replaces inquiry_links + endeavour_links — generalised cross-strand references

_migration.from_v1: Existing Science Y7 node files (y7_science_AC9S7U01.json etc.) use v1 field names. Migrate by renaming: y_goal → progression_endpoint, y_description → progression_checkpoint, xmin → minimum_width, width_core → typical_width, width_enrich_options → wider, inquiry_links + endeavour_links → related_standards.
_migration.dt_nodes: dt_nodes.json uses SOLO taxonomy language (unistructural, multistructural_xmin, relational, extended_abstract). Migrate width fields to minimum_width/typical_width/wider. SOLO labels can be retained as notes if desired but are not required by this schema.
_migration.width_tiers_files: science_y7_width_tiers.json and dt_width_7_8.json already use minimum_width/typical_width/wider. Add strand field when convenient. These files remain separate from node files — standard-level vs node-level width.

_notes.subject_agnostic: This schema is designed to work across Science, Maths, DT, English and future subjects. Subject-specific curriculum codes (ACARA, MYP etc.) are stored as free text strings — no enum constraints.
_notes.mode_A_B: Mode A (concept-certifying) is the default for Understanding strand standards. Mode B (capability-certifying) applies to process/design standards where thinking is only legible through the artefact. Mode selection follows unit intent, not subject label.
_notes.width_principle: Width is the sole differentiation variable. Friction governs width strategy — not the conceptual destination. All students move through the same nodes toward the same progression_endpoint.
_notes.success_criteria_principle: Success criteria are Y-axis certifiers — they are width-agnostic. A student at minimum_width and a student at wider both need to satisfy the same criteria to have reached the node. What differs is the richness and range of contexts in which they demonstrate it.
